prompt: A Heritage Marketplace Operations Management System that includes member tiers, listing discovery, incident handling, and fulfillment controls for a locally hosted cultural crafts and experiences marketplace. Roles include Guest, Member, Seller, Warehouse Staff, Moderator, and Administrator. In the React web UI, Guests can browse listings with keyword search plus filters for neighborhood or radius in miles, price range, layout/square footage where applicable, tags, and availability date windows, then sort by newest, price, distance (manual address-based distance input), or popularity; the system shows recent searches (last 20) and “Trending This Week” based on local activity. Members see their tier card and benefits at checkout and on listing pages, including exclusive pricing, percentage discounts, and free shipping eligibility, with clear on-screen explanations when benefits cannot stack. Sellers and Warehouse Staff manage inventory across multiple warehouses/stores, create inbound/outbound documents, run stocktakes, and receive red “Low Stock” alerts when available units fall below 5. Members can initiate an emergency incident (e.g., delivery dispute, safety concern at pickup) that generates a ticket, supports optional location notes (address and cross-street), and drives SLA timers with escalation to a Moderator if not acknowledged within 15 minutes and not resolved within 24 hours. The system also maintains an exception-and-appeal loop that flags anomalies such as missed pickup check-ins, suspected buddy punching on staff confirmations, or misidentification; users can submit an appeal with locally uploaded evidence (photos/PDFs up to 10 MB each, max 5 files), track status, and receive closure notes.vThe backend uses Spring Boot to expose REST-style APIs consumed by the React frontend, enforcing role-based permissions, signed session tokens, and rate limits (e.g., 60 requests/minute per user, 10 login attempts/hour with a 15-minute lockout). PostgreSQL persists members, tier configurations (spend thresholds such as $0–$499, $500–$1,499, $1,500+), benefit packages with scope controls (listing category, seller, date range), stacking and mutual-exclusion rules, and immutable issuance/redemption records tied to orders and tickets. Inventory logic supports reserved stock during order placement with a 30-minute hold; confirmed orders deduct stock, cancellations or failed fulfillment roll back holds, and every movement is traceable by document, operator, timestamp, and warehouse. Ticketing stores SLA milestones, escalation events, follow-up notes, and closure codes; risk analytics are computed locally from historical tickets and exception flags (e.g., repeat incidents per seller in the last 30 days) and shown as on-prem dashboards without external services. Privacy and security enforce minimal collection and masked display for phone numbers (e.g., 555-***-1234), encrypt sensitive fields at rest, require TLS for local network transmission, support account-level data deletion with a 30-day cooling-off period, and retain audit logs for 2 years for internal compliance. All functions run fully offline with no third-party login, messaging, map, cloud storage, or online payment/refund integrations; any payment and refund references are handled as internal tender records and reconciliation fields only, with idempotent state transitions and manual exception compensation workflows.
